title: Clippy: 5 items declared after statements — reorganize or hoist
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

**Dimension:** Cognitive Readability
**Lint:** clippy::items_after_statements

## Violation
A nested `fn`, `struct`, or `const` appears mid-body after runtime statements. Readers expect items (which exist from scope start regardless of where they're written) to be declared at the top so they can be scanned first.

## Locations
- src/mcp.rs: 220
- src/proxy.rs: 1122
- src/tools/cron.rs: 68, 75, 77

## Mandated Refactor
Move nested items to the top of the enclosing function (or hoist them to module level if they don't capture anything). For helper closures where you want local scope, use a `let helper = |args| ...` binding instead of `fn helper(...)`.

tools/cron.rs has three stacked together (lines 68, 75, 77) suggesting a helper cluster that should be extracted to a dedicated impl/module.
